rulings.Case Title: HAROLD KOEPKE et al VS. FORD MOTOR COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Bifurcation Of Punitive Damages At Trial Pursuant To California Civil Code Section 3295(D)
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, June 11, 2014 in Department 503, Line 3. Defendant Bell Industries, Inc.'s motion for bifurcation of punitive damages at trial pursuant to Civil Code 3295(d) is granted. Plaintiffs do not oppose defendant's motion. The admission of evidence of defendant's profits or financial condition is precluded until after the trier of fact returns a verdict for plaintiffs awarding actual damages and finds that defendant is guilty of malice, oppression, or fraud in accordance with Section 3294. (Civil Code 3295(d). Plaintiffs' request to issue this order as to all other defendants is denied. Plaintiffs' request that defendant be ordered to produce such evidence by the time the verdict is rendered is denied. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: HAROLD KOEPKE et al VS. FORD MOTOR COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication Of Issues
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, June 11, 2014 in Department 503, Line 3. Defendant Bell Industries, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages is granted. As to summary judgment, defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiffs' evidence creates a triable issue whether Mr. Koepke's exposure to asbestos attributable to defendant was, in reasonable medical probability, a substantial factor in causing or contributing to his risk of developing mesothelioma. As to summary adjudication, defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs failed to submit clear and convincing evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: HAROLD KOEPKE et al VS. FORD MOTOR COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, June 11, 2014 in Department 503, Line 3. Defendant Toyota Motor Sales, U.S.A., Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication is granted. As to issues 1 and 2 (fraud/failure to warn and conspiracy/fraud), defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant is liable under plaintiff's fourth or sixth causes of action. As to issue 3 (punitive damages), defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs failed to submit clear and convincing evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiffs' request for a continuance is denied. Plaintiffs failed to submit affidavits demonstrating that facts essential to justify opposition may exist but cannot, for reasons stated, be presented, as required by C.C.P. 437c(h). If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
